hero.description: Physical spaces dedicated to the preservation, study, and profound experience of Kashmir Shaivism. Open to sincere seekers from all over the world.

srinagar.p1: Constructed in 1962 by Swami Lakshmanjoo, this primary Ashram sits majestically in the foothills of the Zaberwan mountains, between the famous Mughal Gardens (Nishat) and Guptaganga, overlooking the Dal Lake.
srinagar.p2Prefix: It was named "Ishwar Ashram" because Swamiji was often referred to as
srinagar.p2Suffix: (a manifestation of God). The grounds house the Amriteshwar Bhairava Temple, inaugurated in 1983, where Swamiji performed daily pooja irrespective of the weather.
srinagar.planDesc: We welcome devotees to stay and immerse themselves in the teachings. Booking is required in advance.
srinagar.caption: Virtual Tour & Ashram Grounds

otherAshrams.title: The Extended Network
otherAshrams.subtitle: Jammu & New Delhi
otherAshrams.note: Note: This ashram is managed independently. Please contact them directly for accommodation availability.

items.desc: A vital center for the Shaiva community, maintaining the Sunday pooja traditions initiated by Swamiji's devotees in the 1970s.
items.address: Ishwar Ashram Trust, Mohalla Paharian, Jammu, J&K

items.desc: Serving the global and metropolitan seekers, the Delhi center facilitates study groups and continuous connection with the philosophy.
items.address: Ishwar Ashram Trust, R-5, Greater Kailash-1, New Delhi

activities.description: Maintaining the rhythm of devotion established by Swami Lakshmanjoo, our centers unite globally through these sacred observances.

items.title: Sunday Puja
items.desc: Traditionally conducted in Swamiji's presence with the recitation of the Sri Gurustuti. Around 1970, Swamiji granted permission for devotees in Jammu and Delhi to also conduct this worship. Today, this unified convention continues across all centers.

items.title: Swamiji's Birthday
items.desc: Celebrated on Vaishakha Krishna Dwadeshi and May 9th. Historically, this was the only time Swamiji would meditate in public, entering Samadhi for hours. The Trust maintains this grand tradition annually with recitations, Prasad, and devotional hymns.

items.title: Mahasamadhi Commemoration
items.desc: Honoring the day the Great Sage of Ishber departed his earthly life on September 27th, 1991. The Trust conducts an annual Havan (fire ceremony) at the Ishwar Ashram in Srinagar to commemorate his eternal presence in the absolute.

community.title: Stay Connected
community.description: All three Ashrams share a unified digital space. Follow the official Facebook page for the latest updates on poojas, events, and daily wisdom from the tradition.

study.title: Explore the Storebook
study.desc: Dive into the definitive translations and original audio lectures of Swami Lakshmanjoo to experience the Trika philosophy directly.

support.title: Preserve the Light
support.desc: Contribute to the ongoing translation of sacred texts, the publication of books, and the maintenance of our sanctuaries.

hero.title: Kashmir Shaiva
hero.description: Dedicated to the threefold science of man and his world. Enabling seekers to rise from individuality to universality, without restriction of caste, creed, or color.

ksi.trikaDescPre: Kashmir Shaivism is known as the pure Trika system. The word Trika means the threefold science. In Trika there are three energies:
ksi.divineQuote: The history goes back to satyuga when Lord Shiva appeared as Svacchandanatha with five heads and eighteen arms, narrating the 64 Bhairava Tantras.
ksi.kstsDesc1: The Kashmir Series of Texts and Studies (KSTS) comprises over 100 volumes, implemented from 1911 onwards by Maharaja Pratap Singh at the request of Swami Ram.
ksi.kstsDesc2: Gathering manuscripts scattered during 500 years of historical shifts, this project became the first printed collection of major Trika texts, ensuring the teachings were preserved for the modern era.

masters.name: Swami Lakshmanjoo
masters.work: The Last Master of the Unbroken Chain

hero.title: Ishwar Ashram
hero.description: Serving metropolitan seekers and facilitating study groups, expanding the reach of the Shaiva tradition to the heart of India.

delhi.bridgeDesc: The Delhi center functions as the vital link for the global Sangha traveling through or residing in India. It ensures that the living tradition of Kashmir Shaivism remains accessible amidst the fast-paced urban environment.
delhi.studyDesc: Hosting regular gatherings where seekers come together to listen to Swami Lakshmanjoo's recorded lectures and study the core philosophies of the Trika system.
delhi.globalDesc: Serving as an administrative hub to facilitate the distribution of sacred texts, event organization, and support for the primary ashram in Srinagar.

hero.title: Ishwar Ashram
hero.description: The epicenter of the tradition, constructed by Swami Lakshmanjoo to preserve and propagate the light of Kashmir Shaivism.

srinagar.p1: Situated in the serene foothills of the Zaberwan mountains, Ishwar Ashram stands as the most significant site for the preservation of Kashmir Shaivism in the modern era.
srinagar.p2: The grounds house the sacred Amriteshwar Bhairava Temple. It was within these walls that Swami Lakshmanjoo held his regular classes, translating dense Tantric texts into accessible wisdom for scholars and seekers from both the East and the West.

hero.description: While our Ashrams provide physical sanctuaries in India, the light of Kashmir Shaivism extends worldwide through our active online community and digital resources.

partnership.description: To ensure seekers worldwide have continuous access to Swamiji's teachings, the Ishwar Ashram Trust works in harmony with the Lakshmanjoo Academy (USA). The Academy hosts the global online study groups, digital pujas, and an extensive library of books, available for purchase internationally.

sangha.title: Free Weekly Sangha
sangha.desc: Join seekers from around the world every week to study the foundational texts and listen to Swamiji's recorded lectures in a supportive online community.

puja.title: Sunday Online Puja
puja.desc: Participate in the traditional Sunday recitation of sacred hymns and verses, maintaining the rhythm of devotion established by Swamiji, broadcasted live.

blog.title: Swamiji Says Blog
blog.desc: Dive into a rich repository of transcribed excerpts, philosophical articles, and short teachings from Swami Lakshmanjoo updated regularly by the Academy.

hero.description: Clarity on our philosophy, the manual process for acquiring sacred texts, and details regarding ashram visits.

faq0.a: Kashmir Shaivism, or Trika philosophy, is a non-dualistic spiritual tradition that emerged in Kashmir. It teaches that the entire universe is a manifestation of pure consciousness (Parama Shiva) and provides practical means (Upayas) to recognize our inherent unity with this divine reality.

faq1.q: Do I need prior background to study the texts?
faq1.a: While the philosophical concepts can be dense, Swami Lakshmanjoo's teachings are uniquely accessible. We recommend starting with 'Kashmir Shaivism: The Secret Supreme' to build a foundational understanding before diving into heavier texts like the Tantraloka.

faq0.q: How does the book purchasing process work?
faq0.a: Our online archive operates on a manual request system to ensure personalized service. When you add a book to your cart and check out, your request is sent to our Ashram team. A staff member will then contact you directly to confirm availability, arrange payment, and coordinate shipping.

faq1.a: Currently, the Ishwar Ashram Trust website only processes and ships book orders within India. For international orders, we kindly redirect you to our official US partner, the Lakshmanjoo Academy, who handles worldwide distribution of Swami Lakshmanjoo's literature.

faq0.q: Can I visit the Ishwar Ashram centers?
faq0.a: Yes. We have centers in Srinagar, Jammu, and New Delhi. Sincere seekers are welcome to visit or participate in our Sunday Poojas. Please contact the specific ashram prior to your visit to confirm timings and accommodation availability.

faq1.q: How do I join an immersive study program?
faq1.a: We periodically organize study retreats in Srinagar. You can find upcoming dates and registration forms on our Workshops page. Spaces are limited to ensure a focused and intimate study environment.

faq0.q: Are there free online resources to learn from?
faq0.a: Absolutely. Our US partner, the Lakshmanjoo Academy, hosts an excellent array of digital resources including free weekly webinars, an online Sangha, and the 'Swamiji Says' blog. We highly recommend exploring their platform for ongoing digital study.

hero.tagline: The Last Master of the Oral Tradition
hero.description: An unbroken chain of supreme awareness, preserved for humanity.

quote.textMain: God and the individual are one.
quote.textEnd: is the essence of Kashmir Shaivism.

biography.p1: Born on May 9, 1907, in Srinagar, Kashmir, Swami Lakshman Joo Raina emerged as the most recent guiding light of the ancient Kashmir Shaiva tradition. His parents, Pandit Narain Dass Raina—a pioneer known for introducing houseboats to Kashmir—and Arnimal, recognized his divine nature early on.
biography.p2: From the tender age of three, his profound spiritual inclination was evident as he spent his days creating and worshipping Shiva-lingams made of clay. This early immersion in divine play foreshadowed a life entirely dedicated to the highest states of consciousness.
biography.p3: He was later initiated by his revered master, Swami Mahatabakak, becoming the ultimate custodian of the profound, non-dualistic Trika philosophy, entrusted with preventing these sacred texts from being lost to time.

items.title: The Auspicious Birth
items.description: Born in Srinagar to a devoted family. His early years were marked by spontaneous states of meditation and an innate understanding of the divine.

items.title: The Unbroken Chain
items.description: Initiated by Swami Mahatabakak, a direct disciple of Swami Ram Tirtha. He immersed himself in the intensive study of the Trika philosophy, mastering the sacred oral tradition.

items.title: The Silent Pilgrimage
items.description: At age 31, Swamiji undertook a 'Maun' (silent) pilgrimage across India. He sought to confirm his spiritual realizations by interacting with other recognized saints.

items.title: Ishber Ashram & Teachings
items.description: Established the ashram in Srinagar. He began holding regular classes for seekers worldwide, translating dense texts into accessible wisdom and writing pivotal papers.

items.title: Mahasamadhi
items.description: Swamiji left his mortal frame, but his presence remains omnipresent. He left behind a vast archive of recorded lectures and clear guidelines to continue his mission.

books.description: Swami Lakshmanjoo dedicated his life to translating and explaining the dense, secretive texts of Kashmir Shaivism. His recorded lectures and published books remain the most authentic gateway to understanding this supreme non-dual reality.
books.mainBookAlt: Kashmir Shaivism: The Secret Supreme Book Cover

hero.descUpcoming: Join us in the sacred valley of Kashmir for an immersive exploration of the mystic dimensions of Trika philosophy.
hero.descDefault: Discover the profound teachings of Kashmir Shaivism through guided immersions, deep textual study, and contemplation in sacred environments.

essence.paragraph: ur immersive programs are designed for sincere seekers who wish to move beyond mere academic study. By stepping away from the routines of daily life, participants are invited to embody the teachings through direct experience, contemplation, and guided discourse.
essence.feature1Desc: Dive deep into the original scriptures of Kashmir Shaivism, guided by the framework and translations of Shaivacharya Swami Lakshmanjoo.
essence.feature2Desc: Many of our programs take place at the foothills of the Mahadeva Mountains in Srinagar, the very birthplace of these ancient teachings.

activeProgram.includesNote: Includes all meals at the Ashram, courseware, and transportation to local shrines. Travel and accommodation are not included.

waitlist.title: Upcoming Immersions
waitlist.description: We are currently preparing the schedule for our next study programs. In the meantime, the most profound preparation is to immerse yourself directly in Swami Lakshmanjoo's translated texts and audio recordings.

hero.description: A supreme monistic philosophy derived from the ancient Bhairava Tantras. It recognizes that the entire universe, and every action within it, is an expression of one infinite consciousness.

quote.quotePrefix: Everything exists in the mirror of God consciousness. Where
quote.author: — Swami Lakshmanjoo

origins.contextDesc: Revealed by Lord Siva in the form of a dialogue between himself and the Divine Mother.
origins.contentPart1Prefix: he teachings of Kashmir Shaivism are derived from the ninety-two Tantras, also known as Agamas. Of these Tantras, sixty-four are considered purely monistic, known as the
origins.contentPart1Suffix: . The essence of these monistic texts is what we call 'Trika Shaivism'.
origins.contentPart2Middle: translates to "the threefold science of man and his world." Within this system, it is recognized that there are three primary energies:
origins.contentPart2AparaLabel: (lowest). This whole universe and every action in it, whether spiritual, physical, or worldly, exists within these three energies.
origins.subtitle: The Secret Supreme
origins.contentPart3: Due to the rise and fall of Kashmir over the past eight centuries, the knowledge of Kashmir Shaivism remained practically hidden, passed down strictly by word of mouth from master to disciple.
origins.contentPart4Prefix: In 1972, Swami Lakshmanjoo—a direct descendant in this unbroken chain of Masters—gave a series of lectures without the aid of texts. Relying solely on his vast memory, he effortlessly verified his statements with quotes from the Shaiva tantras, revealing the essence of the first fifteen chapters of Abhinavagupta's magnum opus, the
origins.contentPart4Middle: . These recordings became the foundational book:

items.title: The 36 Tattvas
items.subtitle: Elements of Creation
items.desc: The nature of the thirty-six elements from earth to Shiva. It maps how the omnipresent, formless state of Parama Shiva descends into limited physical existence, and how it can rise again.

items.title: Pratibimbavada
items.subtitle: Theory of Reflection
items.desc: The universe as we see it is a reflection in the mirror of God consciousness. Created by the absolute independent will of God (svatantrya), the reflection and the reflected are one.

items.title: The Upayas
items.subtitle: The Three Means
items.desc: Three paths for entering Universal God consciousness: shambhavopaya (supreme, no support needed), shaktopaya (medium, centering), and anavopaya (inferior, using objects as aids).

items.title: The Malas
items.subtitle: The Three Impurities
items.desc: The impurities residing in illusion (maya) that bind us: Karma (the impression of pleasure/pain), Mayiya (the creation of duality 'mine/not mine'), and Anava (the feeling of incompleteness).

items.title: Pancakrityavidhi
items.subtitle: The Five Great Acts
items.desc: Creating, protecting, destroying, concealing, and revealing. While the individual soul feels it acts independently, in reality, all actions depend on the will of Lord Shiva.

cta.p1: A devotee once asked Swamiji what was the best way to learn Kashmir Shaivism. His simple reply was:
cta.quote: “Read my Secret Supreme over and over, and you will absorb the essence of Kashmir Shaivism.”
